title: New upper bounds on sphere packings I

thm:1.summary: The general Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound: for any admissible radial $f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}$, not identically zero, satisfying the sign conditions on $f$ and $\widehat{f}$, the center density of $n$-dimensional sphere packings is at most $\frac{f(0)}{2^n\widehat{f}(0)}$ — reducing the geometric packing problem to a function-construction (`magic function`) problem.

thm:2.summary: The magic-function form: if admissible $f$ satisfies (1) $f(0)=\widehat{f}(0)>0$, (2) $f(x)\le 0$ for $|x|\ge r$, and (3) $\widehat{f}(t)\ge 0$ for all $t$, then the center density is at most $(r/2)^n/n!$ — the clean statement Viazovska and CKMRV later realize for $n=8,24$.

thm:3.summary: Delsarte-style bound for spherical codes: if $f(t)=\sum_{j=0}^m a_j P_j(t)$ with $a_j\ge 0$ and $f(t)\le 0$ on $[0,\tau]$, and inner products in $C$ lie in $[0,\tau]$, then $|C|\le f(1)/a_0$ — the discrete LP analogue.

thm:4.summary: Convex-body version: the LP bound extends to packings of translates of a symmetric convex body $C\subset\mathbb{R}^n$, with $f$ admissible and the obvious sign conditions adapted to $C$.

prop:1.summary: Hankel / Bessel-transform formula for radial functions: with $\alpha=n/2-1$, $\widehat{f}(t) = 2\pi|t|^{-\alpha}\int_0^\infty f(r) J_\alpha(2\pi r|t|) r^{n/2}\,dr$ — the analytic identity that converts the radial Fourier transform into a Hankel transform.

prop:2.summary: Explicit Levenshtein-style choice: $f(x) = J_{n/2}(j_{n/2}|x|)^2 / ((1-|x|^2)|x|^n)$ satisfies Theorem 2's hypotheses and yields the upper bound $j_{n/2}^n/((n/2)!^2 4^n)$ for the density.

prop:3.summary: Isodual-lattice variant: a radial admissible $g$ with the stated three properties forces every isodual lattice in dimension $n$ to contain a nonzero vector of length $\le r$.

lem:1.summary: Lattice-from-distance-set lemma: if $S\subset\mathbb{R}^n$ contains $0$, spans $\mathbb{R}^n$, and every pairwise distance is $\sqrt{\text{even integer}}$, then $\langle S\rangle$ is a lattice — the rigidity input for the uniqueness argument.

conjecture:1.summary: Conjectural relationship between the isodual bound (Prop 3) and the magic-function bound (Thm 2): the optimal $r$ values coincide, and the optimal $g,f$ are related by $g=\widehat{f}-f$.

conjecture:2.summary: Magic-function existence: there exist functions satisfying Theorem 2's hypotheses that *solve* the sphere-packing problem in dimensions 2, 8, and 24 — the central conjecture of the program, later confirmed for $n=8$ by Viazovska (1603.04246) and for $n=24$ by CKMRV (1603.06518).

conjecture:3.summary: Convergence of the numerical method: the polynomial-approximation method described in §7 converges as the degree $m\to\infty$ to the optimal bound achievable from Theorem 2.

conjecture:4.summary: Refined magic-function structure: for $n\in\{2,8,24\}$ the optimal $f$ exists and its roots (and those of $\widehat{f}$) occur exactly at the vector lengths of $\Lambda_n$ — predicting the modular-form interpolation structure Viazovska/CKMRV later realize.

edges.note: Theorem 2's magic-function form is the clean special case of the general LP bound (Theorem 1).
The LP bound uses the Hankel/Bessel formula for the radial Fourier transform (Proposition 1).
The convex-body version generalizes the sphere-packing LP bound to packings of translates of a symmetric convex body.
The concrete Levenshtein-style $f$ is an explicit instance of the magic-function hypotheses.
The isodual-lattice variant reuses the LP-duality mechanism of Theorem 1 for the self-dual setting.
Conjecture 1 predicts the magic-function and isodual bounds give the same optimal $r$.
Conjecture 1 links the isodual Proposition 3 to the magic-function Theorem 2.
Conjecture 2 conjectures the existence of magic functions in Theorem 2's sense for $n=2,8,24$.
Conjecture 4 refines Conjecture 2 by predicting the precise root structure of the optimal $f$.
Conjecture 3 predicts the polynomial-approximation numerical method converges to the optimal bound from Theorem 2.

main_results.description: The Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound in its general (Thm 1) and clean magic-function (Thm 2) forms.

variants.description: The Delsarte spherical-codes analogue and the convex-body generalization.

radial_transform.description: The Hankel/Bessel formula for the radial Fourier transform — the analytic tool the LP bound rests on.

concrete_bounds_and_variants.description: Explicit Levenshtein-style bound, the isodual-lattice variant, and the lattice-from-distance-set rigidity input.

magic_function_conjectures.description: The conjectures that drive the program: existence and root-structure of magic functions in dimensions 2, 8, 24 (later proved by Viazovska and CKMRV), the isodual-magic-function correspondence, and the polynomial-method convergence.

ConwaySloane.title: Conway–Sloane — Sphere Packings, Lattices and Groups

Levenshtein.title: V. I. Levenshtein — packing bounds via polynomial method

Delsarte.title: P. Delsarte — LP bounds for codes
